text:
The paper presents the outcome of the ParlaMint II project, which provides a comprehensive set of parliamentary debate corpora, aligned across 29 European countries and autonomous regions. The resulting data set, consisting of parallel corpora for at least the last 7 years (2015-2022), spans over one billion words and is uniformly tokenized. Furthermore, the data set comes with rich meta data for about 24,000 individual speakers. In addition, the whole data set is enriched with a Universal Dependencies syntax annotation and named entity recognition.

Recent developments This section details some of the advancements made in this iteration of the project. They range from developments in the compilation of the corpora and the infrastructure built on GitHub, down to the process of taking each national corpus and running it through a unified pipeline, with the aim of releasing the materials through CLARIN. Data Summary Following the summary of the data, this section then details the qualitative developments of ParlaMint II, including localising metadata, adding political party labels, and automatically switching the language of the files to English with named entity recognition tags. Finally, a pilot speech corpus is released for multimodal projects. Outreach and future work This section details the outreach events and activities for ParlaMint II. Finally, it previews the work to be done in its continuation.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
